definition: Fiscal supply reprices term premium in real-time through observable Treasury auctions, while defaults reprice credit premium with a 12-24 month lag through the maturity wall. This temporal asymmetry means term premium and credit premium can rationally diverge for extended periods in a fiscal-dominated regime.
evidence: Debate pair_3 called this the most analytically sophisticated claim
Term premium responds to observable supply dynamics in real-time
Credit reprices through maturity wall with 12-24 month lag
Explains rational coexistence of high term premium and compressed credit spreads
open_questions: At what term premium level does the lag collapse as conditions overwhelm fiscal support?
Is the 12-24 month credit lag stable or compressing?
